children.heading: Requirement
children.label: a Requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a uniform system to service the Federal credit instruments provided under the CIFIA program.

children.heading: Fees
children.label: b Fees
children.text: If funding sufficient to cover the costs of services of expert firms retained pursuant to subsection (d) and all or a portion of the costs to the Federal Government of servicing the Federal credit instruments is not provided in an appropriations Act for a fiscal year, the Secretary, during that fiscal year, may collect fees on or after the date of the financial close of a Federal credit instrument provided under the CIFIA program at a level that is sufficient to cover those costs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may appoint a financial entity to assist the Secretary in servicing the Federal credit instruments.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 2 Duties
children.text: A servicer appointed under paragraph (1) shall act as the agent for the Secretary.

children.heading: Fee
children.label: 3 Fee
children.text: A servicer appointed under paragraph (1) shall receive a servicing fee, subject to approval by the Secretary.

children.heading: Assistance from expert firms
children.label: d Assistance from expert firms
children.text: The Secretary may retain the services of expert firms, including counsel, in the field of municipal and project finance to assist in the underwriting and servicing of Federal credit instruments.

children.heading: Expedited processing
children.label: e Expedited processing
children.text: The Secretary shall implement procedures and measures to economize the time and cost involved in obtaining approval and the issuance of credit assistance under the CIFIA program.
